reasoning: The base value for 2025-11-23 will be the value from Friday 2025-11-21, which is 2.28%. Recent short-term momentum for the 20-year TIPS yield has been upward, rising from ~2.22% in late October to 2.31% before a slight pullback to 2.28%. Macroeconomic indicators such as strong Treasury issuance, a rising term premium, and sticky inflation risks provide upward pressure on long-term real yields, counteracting the Fed's nominal rate cuts. The FRED short-term linear trend model gives a 69% probability of exceeding 2.28% by late November, though seasonal factors lower the combined estimate closer to 51%. Balancing these, I establish a 61% probability of an increase for the first resolution date. For the more distant horizons, the uncertainties around Fed policy versus structural fiscal deficit impacts mean long-term real rate movements become much harder to predict. Following the instruction to increase uncertainty over time, probabilities regress toward a coin flip (0.50) as the time horizon extends.
